evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Collaborate with Data Engineering to improve data quality
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: Classified as rigour because responsibilities emphasise data quality improvement, maintaining dbt models for the semantic layer, and version control practices, with no velocity signals present.
evidence.domain_risk: Sports betting, gaming and interactive entertainment
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: Classified as high because Entain is a regulated sports betting and iGaming company, placing it in the default-high regulatory compliance sector.
evidence.data_team_maturity: analytics semantic layer; self-serve analytics capabilities
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: Classified as mid because an 'analytics semantic layer' already exists, Data Engineering is a named distinct partner team, and self-serve analytics is an active growth initiative.
evidence.jd_authorship: Extract and analyze large datasets from Snowflake using SQL; Design and maintain dbt models for the analytics semantic layer
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Classified as hiring_manager because specific tools (Snowflake, dbt) are named with precise application context (analytics semantic layer, large dataset querying) — reader understands the Tuesday morning work.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: self-serve analytics capabilities; support team members in dbt and data modeling
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: Classified as internal_data because the primary emphasis is on self-serve analytics, the analytics semantic layer, and upskilling team members — serving the data function itself.
evidence.autonomy_level: Design and maintain dbt models for the analytics semantic layer; Contribute to self-serve analytics capabilities
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Classified as mixed because 'design and maintain' signals strategic ownership of the semantic layer while 'contribute to' and 'support team members' signal execution in service of others.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No AI tool usage or AI infrastructure enablement is mentioned anywhere in the JD.
evidence.testing_framing: Collaborate with Data Engineering to improve data quality
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Classified as tool_listed because data quality is mentioned as a collaboration goal rather than an owned practice with testing tools or frameworks named or assigned as a candidate responsibility.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: Classified as moderate because data quality is a concern surfaced through collaboration with Data Engineering, but the JD is delivery-oriented overall with no compliance or audit framing.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Design and maintain dbt models for the analytics semantic layer
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: Classified as fix_scale because the semantic layer already exists and the role involves maintaining and extending it, not building from zero.
evidence.loss_aversion: improve data quality — data quality is a secondary concern addressed through cross-team collaboration, not a primary trust or compliance driver.
